content.title: Why we need to protect polar bears

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Polar bears are being increasingly threatened by the effects of climate change, but their disappearance could have far-reaching consequences. They are uniquely adapted to the extreme conditions of the Arctic Circle, where temperatures can reach −40°C. One reason for this is that they have up to 11 centimetres of fat underneath their skin. Humans with comparative levels of adipose tissue would be considered obese and would be likely to suffer from diabetes and heart disease. Yet the polar bear experiences no such consequences.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues sheds light on this mystery. They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears. This allowed them to determine the genes that have allowed polar bears to survive in one of the toughest environments on Earth. Liu and his colleagues found the polar bears had a gene known as ApoB, which reduces levels of low-density lipoproteins (LDLs) – a form of 'bad' cholesterol. In humans, mutations of this gene are associated with increased risk of heart disease. Polar bears may therefore be an important study model to understand heart disease in humans.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The genome of the polar bear may also provide the solution for another condition, one that particularly affects our older generation: osteoporosis. This is a disease where bones show reduced density, usually caused by insufficient exercise, reduced calcium intake or food starvation. Bone tissue is constantly being remodelled, meaning that bone is added or removed, depending on nutrient availability and the stress that the bone is under. Female polar bears, however, undergo extreme conditions during every pregnancy. Once autumn comes around, these females will dig maternity dens in the snow and will remain there throughout the winter, both before and after the birth of their cubs. This process results in about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive, depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves. Despite this, their bones remain strong and dense.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Physiologists Alanda Lennox and Allen Goodship found an explanation for this paradox in 2008. They discovered that pregnant bears were able to increase the density of their bones before they started to build their dens. In addition, six months later, when they finally emerged from the den with their cubs, there was no evidence of significant loss of bone density. Hibernating brown bears do not have this capacity and must therefore resort to major bone reformation in the following spring. If the mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears can be understood, many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The medical benefits of the polar bear for humanity certainly have their importance in our conservation efforts, but these should not be the only factors taken into consideration. We tend to want to protect animals we think are intelligent and possess emotions, such as elephants and primates. Bears, on the other hand, seem to be perceived as stupid and in many cases violent. And yet anecdotal evidence from the field challenges those assumptions, suggesting for example that polar bears have good problem-solving abilities. A male bear called GoGo in Tennoji Zoo, Osaka, has even been observed making use of a tool to manipulate his environment. The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach. Problem-solving ability has also been witnessed in wild polar bears, although not as obviously as with GoGo. A calculated move by a male bear involved running and jumping onto barrels in an attempt to get to a photographer standing on a platform four metres high.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: In other studies, such as one by Alison Ames in 2008, polar bears showed deliberate and focused manipulation. For example, Ames observed bears putting objects in piles and then knocking them over in what appeared to be a game. The study demonstrates that bears are capable of agile and thought-out behaviours. These examples suggest bears have greater creativity and problem-solving abilities than previously thought.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: As for emotions, while the evidence is once again anecdotal, many bears have been seen to hit out at ice and snow – seemingly out of frustration – when they have just missed out on a kill. Moreover, polar bears can form unusual relationships with other species, including playing with the dogs used to pull sleds in the Arctic. Remarkably, one hand-raised polar bear called Agee has formed a close relationship with her owner Mark Dumas to the point where they even swim together. This is even more astonishing since polar bears are known to actively hunt humans in the wild.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: If climate change were to lead to their extinction, this would mean the loss not only of potential breakthroughs in human medicine, but more importantly, the disappearance of an intelligent, majestic animal.

groups.desc:
Questions 1–7

Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In questions1–7, choose
TRUE if the statement agrees with the information
FALSE if the statement contradicts the information
NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

questions.title: Polar bears suffer from various health problems due to the build-up of fat under their skin.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“suffer from various health problems due to the build-up of fat under their skin”意思是“北极熊由于皮下脂肪堆积而遭受各种健康问题”。而原文中明确指出“Yet the polar bear experiences no such consequences”，意思是“北极熊并没有这样的后果”。这表明北极熊并没有因为脂肪堆积而遭受健康问题。因此，题干中的陈述与原文内容相反，所以答案是FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: Yet the polar bear experiences no such consequences.
central_sentences.translation: 然而，北极熊并没有经历这些后果。

target_sen.target_sen: Yet the polar bear experiences no such consequences.

syn_ary.synonym: 11 centimetres of fat underneath their skin

target_sen.target_sen: One reason for this is that they have up to 11 centimetres of fat underneath their skin.

locating_words.locating_word: build-up of fat under their skin

questions.title: The study done by Liu and his colleagues compared different groups of polar bears.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "different groups of polar bears" 表示不同组的北极熊，而原文中提到的是北极熊和棕熊之间的比较。题干中的信息与原文不符，因此答案是 FALSE。学生需要注意题干和原文中的比较对象是否一致。

central_sentences.sentence: They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.
central_sentences.translation: 他们比较了北极熊的基因结构与其来自较温暖气候的最近亲属棕熊的基因结构。

syn_ary.synonym: polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears

target_sen.target_sen: They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.
A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues sheds light on this mystery.
Polar bears may therefore be an important study model to understand heart disease in humans.
A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues sheds light on this mystery.

questions.title: Liu and colleagues were the first researchers to compare polar bears and brown bears genetically.
questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词“first researchers”在原文中没有找到对应的同义替换词。虽然原文提到刘世平及其同事进行了基因比较，但没有提到他们是第一个进行这种比较的研究者。因此，题干中的信息在文章中找不到，正确答案是“NOT GIVEN”。

central_sentences.sentence: A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues sheds light on this mystery. They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.
central_sentences.translation: 2014年，施平刘和他的同事们进行的一项研究揭示了这个谜题。他们比较了北极熊与其最近的亲戚——来自较温暖气候的棕熊的基因结构。

target_sen.target_sen: A 2014 study by Shi Ping Liu and colleagues sheds light on this mystery.
They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.

syn_ary.synonym: polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears

target_sen.target_sen: They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.
They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.

questions.title: Polar bears are able to control their levels of 'bad' cholesterol by genetic means.
questions.analysis: 题干中的信息“Polar bears are able to control their levels of 'bad' cholesterol by genetic means”在原文中得到了明确的对应。原文提到“polar bears had a gene known as APoB, which reduces levels of low-density lipoproteins (LDLs) – a form of 'bad' cholesterol”，这表明北极熊通过基因（APoB）来降低“坏”胆固醇（LDLs）的水平。因此，题干中的信息是正确的，答案为TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: Liu and his colleagues found the polar bears had a gene known as APoB, which reduces levels of low-density lipoproteins (LDLs) – a form of 'bad' cholesterol.
central_sentences.translation: Liu和他的同事们发现北极熊有一种名为APoB的基因，这种基因可以降低低密度脂蛋白（LDLs）的水平——这是一种"坏"胆固醇。

target_sen.target_sen: They compared the genetic structure of polar bears with that of their closest relatives from a warmer climate, the brown bears.
This allowed them to determine the genes that have allowed polar bears to survive in one of the toughest environments on Earth.
Liu and his colleagues found the polar bears had a gene known as APoB, which reduces levels of low-density lipoproteins (LDLs) – a form of 'bad' cholesterol.

questions.title: Female polar bears are able to survive for about six months without food.
questions.analysis: 题干中的“Female polar bears are able to survive for about six months without food”与原文中的“about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive”是同义表达。题干中的“without food”对应原文中的“fasting”，“six months”在题干和原文中都是相同的表达，“Female polar bears”对应原文中的“female bears”。因此，题干信息在原文中可以找到明确的对应表达，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: This process results in about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive, depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves.
central_sentences.translation: 这个过程导致大约六个月的禁食，在此期间，雌性北极熊必须维持自己和幼崽的生存，消耗自身的钙质和热量储备。

target_sen.target_sen: This process results in about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive, depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves.
This process results in about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive, depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves.
This process results in about six months of fasting, where the female bears have to keep themselves and their cubs alive, depleting their own calcium and calorie reserves.

questions.title: It was found that the bones of female polar bears were very weak when they came out of their dens in spring.
questions.analysis: 题干中的 "bones of female polar bears were very weak" 与原文中的 "no evidence of significant loss of bone density" 是相反的信息。题干说骨头很弱，而原文明确指出没有显著的骨密度损失。因此，答案是 FALSE。

central_sentences.sentence: In addition, six months later, when they finally emerged from the den with their cubs, there was no evidence of significant loss of bone density.
central_sentences.translation: 此外，六个月后，当它们最终带着幼崽从巢穴中出来时，没有证据表明骨密度有显著损失。

target_sen.target_sen: They discovered that pregnant bears were able to increase the density of their bones before they started to build their dens.

syn_ary.synonym: no evidence of significant loss of bone density

target_sen.target_sen: In addition, six months later, when they finally emerged from the den with their cubs, there was no evidence of significant loss of bone density.
In addition, six months later, when they finally emerged from the den with their cubs, there was no evidence of significant loss of bone density.

questions.title: The polar bear's mechanism for increasing bone density could also be used by people one day.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是TRUE。题干中的“mechanism for increasing bone density”在原文中对应的是“mechanism of bone remodelling”，“people”对应的是“humans”和“astronauts”。原文明确提到“many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit”，这与题干中的“could also be used by people one day”意思一致。因此，答案是TRUE。

central_sentences.sentence: If the mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears can be understood, many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit.
central_sentences.translation: 如果能够理解北极熊骨骼重塑的机制，许多卧床的人类，甚至宇航员都可能从中受益。

syn_ary.synonym: mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears

target_sen.target_sen: If the mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears can be understood, many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit.

syn_ary.synonym: many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit

target_sen.target_sen: If the mechanism of bone remodelling in polar bears can be understood, many bedridden humans, and even astronauts, could potentially benefit.

groups.title: Reasons why polar bears should be protected
groups.desc:
Questions 8-13

Complete the table below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 8–13.
groups.collect:
People think of bears as unintelligent and $328$.
However, this may not be correct. For example:
• In Tennoji Zoo, a bear has been seen using a branch as a $329$This allowed him to knock down some $330$.
• A wild polar bear worked out a method of reaching a platform where a$331$was located.
• Polar bears have displayed behaviour such as conscious manipulation of objects and activity similar to a $332$
Bears may also display emotions. For example:
• They may make movements suggesting $333$if disappointed when hunting.
• They may form relationships with other species.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "unintelligent" 对应原文中的 "stupid"，而 "and" 连接两个形容词，说明需要找两个描述熊的词汇。原文中 "stupid and in many cases violent" 直接对应题干中的 "unintelligent and 【 】"，因此答案是 "violent"。这个答案是正确的，因为它准确地反映了原文中对熊的负面描述。

central_sentences.sentence: Bears, on the other hand, seem to be perceived as stupid and in many cases violent.
central_sentences.translation: 另一方面，人们似乎认为熊是愚蠢的，在许多情况下还是暴力的。

target_sen.target_sen: Bears, on the other hand, seem to be perceived as stupid and in many cases violent.

questions.analysis: 答案是 "tool"。题干中的 "In Tennoji Zoo" 和 "using a branch" 对应原文中的 "Tennoji Zoo, Osaka" 和 "used a tree branch"。原文明确提到 "making use of a tool"，说明树枝被用作工具。因此，正确答案是 "tool"。

central_sentences.sentence: A male bear called GoGo in Tennoji Zoo, Osaka, has even been observed making use of a tool to manipulate his environment. The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach.
central_sentences.translation: 在大阪的天王寺动物园，一只名叫GoGo的雄性熊被观察到使用工具来操控周围环境。这只熊多次使用树枝来取下挂在高处够不着的肉。

target_sen.target_sen: A male bear called GoGo in Tennoji Zoo, Osaka, has even been observed making use of a tool to manipulate his environment.
The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach.

questions.analysis: 题干中的线索词 "In Tennoji Zoo" 对应文章中的 "Tennoji Zoo, Osaka"，"using a branch" 对应 "used a tree branch"，"knock down" 对应 "dislodge"。通过这些对应关系，可以定位到文章中的句子 "The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach."。从这句话中可以看出，熊使用树枝敲掉的是一块肉，因此答案是 "meat"。

central_sentences.sentence: The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach.
central_sentences.translation: 这只熊多次使用树枝来弄下悬挂在他无法够到的地方的一块肉。

target_sen.target_sen: A male bear called GoGo in Tennoji Zoo, Osaka, has even been observed making use of a tool to manipulate his environment.

The bear used a tree branch on multiple occasions to dislodge a piece of meat hung out of his reach.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“polar bear”、“platform”和“where”在原文中都有直接对应的同义词。原文句子描述了北极熊通过跑步和跳上桶来达到一个站在平台上的摄影师。题干问的是北极熊试图达到的平台上有什么，答案是“photographer”，因为这是平台上唯一提到的对象。

central_sentences.sentence: A calculated move by a male bear involved running and jumping onto barrels in an attempt to get to a photographer standing on a platform four metres high.
central_sentences.translation: 一只雄性北极熊做出了一个精心计算的举动，它跑跳到桶上，试图接近站在四米高平台上的摄影师。

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "conscious manipulation of objects" 对应文章中的 "putting objects in piles and then knocking them over"，而 "activity similar to" 对应文章中的 "in what appeared to be"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确定答案所在的句子是 "For example, Ames observed bears putting objects in piles and then knocking them over in what appeared to be a game."。因此，答案是 "game"。

central_sentences.sentence: For example, Ames observed bears putting objects in piles and then knocking them over in what appeared to be a game.
central_sentences.translation: 例如，艾姆斯观察到熊把物体堆成堆，然后将它们推倒，这看起来像是一种游戏。

questions.analysis: 题干中的“disappointed”对应原文中的“missed out on a kill”，而“make movements suggesting”对应“hit out at ice and snow”。原文中明确提到这些行为是“seemingly out of frustration”，即“似乎是因为沮丧”。因此，正确答案是“frustration”。

central_sentences.sentence: As for emotions, while the evidence is once again anecdotal, many bears have been seen to hit out at ice and snow – seemingly out of frustration – when they have just missed out on a kill.
central_sentences.translation: 关于情感方面，虽然证据再次是轶事性的，但人们已经观察到许多熊在刚刚错过猎物时会击打冰雪 - 似乎是出于沮丧。

content.title: The Step Pyramid of Djoser

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: The pyramids are the most famous monuments of ancient Egypt and still hold enormous interest for people in the present day. These grand, impressive tributes to the memory of the Egyptian kings have become linked with the country even though other cultures, such as the Chinese and Mayan, also built pyramids. The evolution of the pyramid form has been written and argued about for centuries. However, there is no question that, as far as Egypt is concerned, it began with one monument to one king designed by one brilliant architect: the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Djoser was the first king of the Third Dynasty of Egypt and the first to build in stone. Prior to Djoser's reign, tombs were rectangular monuments made of dried clay brick, which covered underground passages where the deceased person was buried. For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid. Djoser is thought to have reigned for 19 years, but some historians and scholars attribute a much longer time for his rule, owing to the number and size of the monuments he built.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: The Step Pyramid has been thoroughly examined and investigated over the last century, and it is now known that the building process went through many different stages. Historian Marc Van de Mieroop comments on this, writing 'Much experimentation was involved, which is especially clear in the construction of the pyramid in the center of the complex. It had several plans … before it became the first Step Pyramid in history, piling six levels on top of one another … The weight of the enormous mass was a challenge for the builders, who placed the stones at an inward incline in order to prevent the monument breaking up.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: When finally completed, the Step Pyramid rose 62 meters high and was the tallest structure of its time. The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests. It covered a region of 16 hectares and was surrounded by a wall 10.5 meters high. The wall had 13 false doors cut into it with only one true entrance cut into the south-east corner; the entire wall was then ringed by a trench 750 meters long and 40 meters wide. The false doors and the trench were incorporated into the complex to discourage unwanted visitors. If someone wished to enter, he or she would have needed to know in advance how to find the location of the true opening in the wall. Djoser was so proud of his accomplishment that he broke the tradition of having only his own name on the monument and had Imhotep's name carved on it as well.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The burial chamber of the tomb, where the king's body was laid to rest, was dug beneath the base of the pyramid, surrounded by a vast maze of long tunnels that had rooms off them to discourage robbers. One of the most mysterious discoveries found inside the pyramid was a large number of stone vessels. Over 40,000 of these vessels, of various forms and shapes, were discovered in storerooms off the pyramid's underground passages. They are inscribed with the names of rulers from the First and Second Dynasties of Egypt and made from different kinds of stone. There is no agreement among scholars and archaeologists on why the vessels were placed in the tomb of Djoser or what they were supposed to represent. The archaeologist Jean-Philippe Lauer, who excavated most of the pyramid and complex, believes they were originally stored and then given a 'proper burial' by Djoser in his pyramid to honor his predecessors. There are other historians, however, who claim the vessels were dumped into the shafts as yet another attempt to prevent grave robbers from getting to the king's burial chamber.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Unfortunately, all of the precautions and intricate design of the underground network did not prevent ancient robbers from finding a way in. Djoser's grave goods, and even his body, were stolen at some point in the past and all archaeologists found were a small number of his valuables overlooked by the thieves. There was enough left throughout the pyramid and its complex, however, to astonish and amaze the archaeologists who excavated it.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Egyptologist Miroslav Verner writes, 'Few monuments hold a place in human history as significant as that of the Step Pyramid in Saqqara … It can be said without exaggeration that this pyramid complex constitutes a milestone in the evolution of monumental stone architecture in Egypt and in the world as a whole.' The Step Pyramid was a revolutionary advance in architecture and became the archetype which all the other great pyramid builders of Egypt would follow.

groups.title: List of Headings
groups.desc:
Questions 14–20

Reading Passage 2 has seven paragraphs, A–G.
Choose the correct heading for each paragraph and move it into the gap.

list.text: The areas and artefacts within the pyramid itself
A difficult task for those involved
A king who saved his people
A single certainty among other less definite facts
An overview of the external buildings and areas
A pyramid design that others copied
An idea for changing the design of burial structures
An incredible experience despite the few remains
The answers to some unexpected questions

questions.analysis: Section A的内容主要讲述了金字塔的广泛影响和吸引力，以及关于金字塔形态演变的争论。关键句子指出，尽管有很多争论，但有一个确定的事实是埃及金字塔起源于Djoser的阶梯金字塔。这与小标题iv: A single certainty among other less definite facts完全匹配，因为它强调了一个确定的事实和其他不确定的争论。

central_sentences.sentence: The evolution of the pyramid form has been written and argued about for centuries. However, there is no question that, as far as Egypt is concerned, it began with one monument to one king designed by one brilliant architect: the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara.
central_sentences.translation: 关于金字塔形态的演变已经被讨论和争论了几个世纪。然而，就埃及而言，毫无疑问它始于一位杰出建筑师为一位国王设计的一座纪念碑：萨卡拉的乔塞尔阶梯金字塔。

target_sen.target_sen: However, there is no question that, as far as Egypt is concerned, it began with one monument to one king designed by one brilliant architect: the Step Pyramid of Djoser at Saqqara.

syn_ary.synonym: has been written and argued about for centuries

target_sen.target_sen: The evolution of the pyramid form has been written and argued about for centuries.

questions.analysis: 这段内容主要讲述了伊姆霍特普提出了一个新的想法，改变了墓葬结构的设计，从原来的矩形砖墓变成了阶梯金字塔。这与小标题vii: An idea for changing the design of burial structures的意思完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid.
central_sentences.translation: 由于一些尚不清楚的原因，焦塞尔的主要官员伊姆霍特普构想出为国王建造一座更高、更令人印象深刻的墓葬，方法是将石板一层层堆叠，逐渐变小，形成了现在所知的阶梯金字塔的形状。

target_sen.target_sen: For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid.

For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid.

For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid.
Prior to Djoser's reign, tombs were rectangular monuments made of dried clay brick, which covered underground passages where the deceased person was buried.

For reasons which remain unclear, Djoser's main official, whose name was Imhotep, conceived of building a taller, more impressive tomb for his king by stacking stone slabs on top of one another, progressively making them smaller, to form the shape now known as the Step Pyramid.

questions.analysis: 这段文字描述了建造阶梯金字塔的过程中遇到的挑战和试验，特别提到“挑战”（challenge）和“许多试验”（experimentation），这些都表明这是一个困难的任务。因此，小标题“ii: A difficult task for those involved”是最合适的。

central_sentences.sentence: The weight of the enormous mass was a challenge for the builders, who placed the stones at an inward incline in order to prevent the monument breaking up.
central_sentences.translation: 巨大的重量对建筑者来说是一个挑战，他们将石块向内倾斜放置，以防止纪念碑崩塌。

target_sen.target_sen: It had several plans … before it became the first Step Pyramid in history, piling six levels on top of one another … The weight of the enormous mass was a challenge for the builders, who placed the stones at an inward incline in order to prevent the monument breaking up.'
Historian Marc Van de Mieroop comments on this, writing 'Much experimentation was involved, which is especially clear in the construction of the pyramid in the center of the complex.

It had several plans … before it became the first Step Pyramid in history, piling six levels on top of one another … The weight of the enormous mass was a challenge for the builders, who placed the stones at an inward incline in order to prevent the monument breaking up.'

questions.analysis: Section D详细描述了Step Pyramid的外部建筑和区域，包括庙宇、庭院、神殿和祭司的居住区。这些内容与小标题v "An overview of the external buildings and areas"（外部建筑和区域的概述）直接对应。因此，Section D对应的小标题是v。

central_sentences.sentence: The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.
central_sentences.translation: 这座建筑群的规模相当于古埃及的一座城市，包括庙宇、庭院、神殿和祭司的居住区。

syn_ary.synonym: temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests

target_sen.target_sen: The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.
The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.
The false doors and the trench were incorporated into the complex to discourage unwanted visitors.
It covered a region of 16 hectares and was surrounded by a wall 10.5 meters high.

questions.analysis: Section E详细描述了金字塔内部的区域和文物，包括墓室、隧道、房间和石器。这些内容直接对应了小标题i: "The areas and artefacts within the pyramid itself"。因此，Section E的最佳标题是i。

central_sentences.sentence: The burial chamber of the tomb, where the king's body was laid to rest, was dug beneath the base of the pyramid, surrounded by a vast maze of long tunnels that had rooms off them to discourage robbers.
central_sentences.translation: 墓室位于金字塔底部之下，国王的遗体安放在这里，周围是一个庞大的长隧道迷宫，隧道两侧有房间，目的是阻止盗墓贼。

central_sentences.sentence: One of the most mysterious discoveries found inside the pyramid was a large number of stone vessels. Over 40,000 of these vessels, of various forms and shapes, were discovered in storerooms off the pyramid's underground passages.
central_sentences.translation: 在金字塔内部发现的最神秘的东西之一是大量的石器。在金字塔地下通道旁的储藏室里发现了超过40,000件各种形状的石器。

target_sen.target_sen: The burial chamber of the tomb, where the king's body was laid to rest, was dug beneath the base of the pyramid, surrounded by a vast maze of long tunnels that had rooms off them to discourage robbers.
There are other historians, however, who claim the vessels were dumped into the shafts as yet another attempt to prevent grave robbers from getting to the king's burial chamber.

questions.analysis: Section F的内容描述了尽管Djoser的墓被盗，考古学家们仍然发现了一些遗留的贵重物品，这些物品足以让他们感到惊讶和惊奇。这与小标题viii "An incredible experience despite the few remains"（尽管只剩下少量遗物，但仍然是一段令人难以置信的经历）直接对应。因此，Section F对应的小标题是viii。

central_sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, all of the precautions and intricate design of the underground network did not prevent ancient robbers from finding a way in. Djoser's grave goods, and even his body, were stolen at some point in the past and all archaeologists found were a small number of his valuables overlooked by the thieves. There was enough left throughout the pyramid and its complex, however, to astonish and amaze the archaeologists who excavated it.
central_sentences.translation: 不幸的是，地下网络的所有预防措施和复杂设计都没能阻止古代盗墓者找到进入的方法。乔塞尔的陪葬品，甚至他的身体，在过去的某个时候都被盗走了，考古学家发现的只是被盗贼遗漏的少量贵重物品。然而，金字塔及其建筑群中仍然留有足够的东西，令挖掘它的考古学家感到惊讶和惊叹。

target_sen.target_sen: There was enough left throughout the pyramid and its complex, however, to astonish and amaze the archaeologists who excavated it.

syn_ary.synonym: a small number of his valuables

target_sen.target_sen: Djoser's grave goods, and even his body, were stolen at some point in the past and all archaeologists found were a small number of his valuables overlooked by the thieves.

questions.analysis: Section G的主要内容是描述阶梯金字塔在建筑史上的重要性和它作为后续金字塔建造者模范的角色。因此，小标题vi: A pyramid design that others copied是最合适的选择。通过识别关键的同义表达和理解文章的主要内容，学生可以更准确地选择正确的小标题。

central_sentences.sentence: The Step Pyramid was a revolutionary advance in architecture and became the archetype which all the other great pyramid builders of Egypt would follow.
central_sentences.translation: 阶梯金字塔是建筑史上的一项革命性进步，成为了所有其他埃及伟大金字塔建造者效仿的典范。

target_sen.target_sen: The Step Pyramid was a revolutionary advance in architecture and became the archetype which all the other great pyramid builders of Egypt would follow.

groups.title: The Step Pyramid of Djoser
groups.desc:
Questions 21–24

Complete the notes below.
Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
Write your answers in boxes 21–24.
groups.collect:
The complex that includes the Step Pyramid and its surroundings is considered to be as big as an Egyptian $341$ of the past. The area outside the pyramid included accommodation that was occupied by $342$ , along with many other buildings and features.
A wall ran around the outside of the complex and a number of false entrances were built into this. In addition, a long $343$ encircled the wall. As a result, any visitors who had not been invited were cleverly prevented from entering the pyramid grounds unless they knew the $344$ of the real entrance.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“complex”在原文中直接对应“complex”，“as big as”对应“the size of”，“an Egyptian 【 】 of the past”对应“a city in ancient Egypt”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案是“city”，因为它符合题干中表示大小对比的逻辑。

central_sentences.sentence: The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.
central_sentences.translation: 这个建筑群的规模相当于古埃及的一座城市，包括一座神庙、庭院、神龛和祭司的生活区。

target_sen.target_sen: The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.
The false doors and the trench were incorporated into the complex to discourage unwanted visitors.
It covered a region of 16 hectares and was surrounded by a wall 10.5 meters high.

questions.analysis: 题干中的 "accommodation" 对应文章中的 "living quarters"，"occupied by" 对应文章中的 "for"。通过这些同义表达，我们可以确认答案在 "living quarters for the priests" 这部分。因此，答案是 "priests"。题干问的是谁住在这些居住区，而文章明确提到这些居住区是为祭司准备的。

central_sentences.sentence: The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.

The complex in which it was built was the size of a city in ancient Egypt and included a temple, courtyards, shrines, and living quarters for the priests.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“encircled the wall”与原文中的“ringed by”是同义表达，且“long”在原文中对应“750 meters long”。通过这些线索词的对应关系，我们可以确定答案所在的句子。句子中明确提到“ringed by a trench”，因此答案是“trench”。

central_sentences.sentence: the entire wall was then ringed by a trench 750 meters long and 40 meters wide. The
central_sentences.translation: 整个墙壁随后被一条750米长、40米宽的壕沟环绕。

The wall had 13 false doors cut into it with only one true entrance cut into the south-east corner; the entire wall was then ringed by a trench 750 meters long and 40 meters wide.
If someone wished to enter, he or she would have needed to know in advance how to find the location of the true opening in the wall.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“visitors who had not been invited”对应原文中的“unwanted visitors”，“real entrance”对应“true opening”，“knew”对应“would have needed to know”。通过这些对应关系，可以确定答案句中的关键词是“location”，因为它准确地回答了题干中访客需要知道什么的问题。

groups.title: Which TWO of the following points does the writer make about King Djoser?
groups.desc:
Questions 25–26

Choose TWO letters, A–E.
Choose the correct letters for questions25 and 26.

collect_option.title: Which TWO of the following points does the writer make about King Djoser?

list.text: Initially he had to be persuaded to build in stone rather than clay.
There is disagreement concerning the length of his reign.
He failed to appreciate Imhotep's part in the design of the Step Pyramid.
A few of his possessions were still in his tomb when archaeologists found it.
He criticised the design and construction of other pyramids in Egypt.

questions.analysis: 根据题目中的关键词'make'和'points'，以及段落中对应的关键词'is thought'、'conceived of building'、'attribute'和'were stolen'等，可以看出题目要求选出作者提出的两个关于Djoser国王的论点。从段落可以看出, B选项'there is disagreement concerning the length of his reign'和D选项'a few of his possessions were still in his tomb when archaeologists found it'是正确答案,因为段落中提到Djoser统治时间有争议,以及他的一些随葬品被盗但仍有部分遗留下来。

central_sentences.sentence: Djoser is thought to have reigned for 19 years, but some historians and scholars attribute a much longer time for his rule, owing to the number and size of the monuments he built.
central_sentences.translation: 据认为乔塞尔统治了19年，但是一些历史学家和学者认为他的统治时间更长，这是基于他建造的纪念碑的数量和规模。

central_sentences.sentence: Djoser's grave goods, and even his body, were stolen at some point in the past and all archaeologists found were a small number of his valuables overlooked by the thieves.
central_sentences.translation: 乔塞尔的陪葬品，甚至他的遗体，在过去的某个时候被盗走了，考古学家只找到了一小部分被盗贼忽视的贵重物品。

These grand, impressive tributes to the memory of the Egyptian kings have become linked with the country even though other cultures, such as the Chinese and Mayan, also built pyramids.

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: According to a leading business consultancy, 3–14% of the global workforce will need to switch to a different occupation within the next 10–15 years, and all workers will need to adapt as their occupations evolve alongside increasingly capable machines. Automation – or 'embodied artificial intelligence' (AI) – is one aspect of the disruptive effects of technology on the labour market. 'Disembodied AI', like the algorithms running in our smartphones, is another.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy. Algorithms are capable of learning from data to undertake tasks that previously needed human judgement, such as reading legal contracts, analysing medical scans and gathering market intelligence.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: 'In many cases, they can outperform humans,' says Pachidi. 'Organisations are attracted to using algorithms because they want to make choices based on what they consider is “perfect information”, as well as to reduce costs and enhance productivity.'

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: 'But these enhancements are not without consequences,' says Pachidi. 'If routine cognitive tasks are taken over by AI, how do professions develop their future experts?' she asks. 'One way of learning about a job is “legitimate peripheral participation” – a novice stands next to experts and learns by observation. If this isn't happening, then you need to find new ways to learn.'

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Another issue is the extent to which the technology influences or even controls the workforce. For over two years, Pachidi monitored a telecommunications company. 'The way telecoms salespeople work is through personal and frequent contact with clients, using the benefit of experience to assess a situation and reach a decision. However, the company had started using an algorithm that defined when account managers should contact certain customers about which kinds of campaigns and what to offer them.'

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The algorithm – usually built by external designers – often becomes the keeper of knowledge, she explains. In cases like this, Pachidi believes, a short-sighted view begins to creep into working practices whereby workers learn through the 'algorithm's eyes' and become dependent on its instructions. Alternative explorations – where experimentation and human instinct lead to progress and new ideas – are effectively discouraged.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Pachidi and colleagues even observed people developing strategies to make the algorithm work to their own advantage. 'We are seeing cases where workers feed the algorithm with false data to reach their targets,' she reports.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: It's scenarios like these that many researchers are working to avoid. Their objective is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent, so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made. In the meantime, says Pachidi, 'We need to make sure we fully understand the dilemmas that this new world raises regarding expertise, occupational boundaries and control.'

paragraphs.label: I
paragraphs.text: Economist Professor Hamish Low believes that the future of work will involve major transitions across the whole life course for everyone: 'The traditional trajectory of full-time education followed by full-time work followed by a pensioned retirement is a thing of the past,' says Low. Instead, he envisages a multistage employment life: one where retraining happens across the life course, and where multiple jobs and no job happen by choice at different stages.

paragraphs.label: J
paragraphs.text: On the subject of job losses, Low believes the predictions are founded on a fallacy: 'It assumes that the number of jobs is fixed. If in 30 years, half of 100 jobs are being carried out by robots, that doesn't mean we are left with just 50 jobs for humans. The number of jobs will increase: we would expect there to be 150 jobs.'

paragraphs.label: K
paragraphs.text: Dr Ewan McGaughey, at Cambridge's Centre for Business Research and King's College London, agrees that 'apocalyptic' views about the future of work are misguided. 'It's the laws that restrict the supply of capital to the job market, not the advent of new technologies that causes unemployment.'

paragraphs.label: L
paragraphs.text: His recently published research answers the question of whether automation, AI and robotics will mean a 'jobless future' by looking at the causes of unemployment. 'History is clear that change can mean redundancies. But social policies can tackle this through retraining and redeployment.'

paragraphs.label: M
paragraphs.text: He adds: 'If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security. We can “reprogramme” the law to prepare for a fairer future of work and leisure.' McGaughey's findings are a call to arms to leaders of organisations, governments and banks to pre-empt the coming changes with bold new policies that guarantee full employment, fair incomes and a thriving economic democracy.

paragraphs.label: N
paragraphs.text: 'The promises of these new technologies are astounding. They deliver humankind the capacity to live in a way that nobody could have once imagined,' he adds. 'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced. But it will not only be one of technology. The next revolution will be social.'

groups.desc:
Questions 27–30

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.title: The first paragraph tells us about

options.text: the kinds of jobs that will be most affected by the growth of AI.
the extent to which AI will alter the nature of the work that people do.
the proportion of the world's labour force who will have jobs in AI in the future.
the difference between ways that embodied and disembodied AI will impact on workers.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B，因为文章明确提到AI将如何改变人们的工作性质。具体来说，文章提到一部分劳动力需要转行，所有工人都需要适应他们的职业演变，这些都表明了AI对工作性质的改变程度。

central_sentences.sentence: 3–14% of the global workforce will need to switch to a different occupation within the next 10–15 years, and all workers will need to adapt as their occupations evolve alongside increasingly capable machines.
central_sentences.translation: 在未来10-15年内，全球3-14%的劳动力需要转换到不同的职业，而所有工人都需要适应他们的职业随着越来越有能力的机器而演变。

questions.title: According to the second paragraph, what is Stella Pachidi's view of the 'knowledge economy'?

options.text: It is having an influence on the number of jobs available.
It is changing people's attitudes towards their occupations.
It is the main reason why the production sector is declining.
It is a key factor driving current developments in the workplace.

questions.analysis: Stella Pachidi认为，知识经济导致了一些最基本的变化。这些变化是由于依赖数据而不是生产的工作的“算法化”引起的。选项D提到“它是推动当前工作场所发展的关键因素”，与文章中提到的“fundamental changes”直接对应。因此，选项D是正确答案。

central_sentences.sentence: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy.
central_sentences.translation: 剑桥大学贾奇商学院的Stella Pachidi博士认为，一些最根本的变化正在发生，这是由于依赖数据而非生产的工作的"算法化"所导致的——这就是所谓的知识经济。

target_sen.target_sen: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy.

questions.title: What did Pachidi observe at the telecommunications company?

options.text: staff disagreeing with the recommendations of AI
staff feeling resentful about the intrusion of AI in their work
staff making sure that AI produces the results that they want
staff allowing AI to carry out tasks they ought to do themselves

questions.analysis: 正确答案是C，因为文章中明确提到员工通过操控算法（例如输入虚假数据）来确保AI产生他们想要的结果。这与选项C的描述完全一致。

central_sentences.sentence: Pachidi and colleagues even observed people developing strategies to make the algorithm work to their own advantage. 'We are seeing cases where workers feed the algorithm with false data to reach their targets,' she reports.
central_sentences.translation: Pachidi和她的同事甚至观察到人们开发策略使算法为自己的利益服务。她报告说："我们看到有些工人向算法输入虚假数据以达到他们的目标。"

target_sen.target_sen: Pachidi and colleagues even observed people developing strategies to make the algorithm work to their own advantage.

questions.title: In his recently published research, Ewan McGaughey

options.text: challenges the idea that redundancy is a negative thing.
shows the profound effect of mass unemployment on society.
highlights some differences between past and future job losses.
illustrates how changes in the job market can be successfully handled.

questions.analysis: McGaughey的研究强调了通过政策改进来应对工作市场的变化，确保就业和经济的稳定。这与题干所说的“如何成功应对工作市场的变化”是对应的。因此，正确答案是D。

central_sentences.sentence: If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security. We can “reprogramme” the law to prepare for a fairer future of work and leisure.' McGaughey
central_sentences.translation: 如果AI和机器人技术将导致工作岗位发生变化，那么我希望看到政府抓住机会改进政策，以确保良好的工作保障。我们可以"重新编程"法律，为更公平的工作和休闲未来做准备。

groups.title: The 'algorithmication' of jobs
groups.desc:
Questions 31–34

Complete the summary using the list of words, A–G, below.
Movethe correct answer, A–G, into boxes 31–34.
groups.collect:
Stella Pachidi of Cambridge Judge Business School has been focusing on the 'algorithmication' of jobs which rely not on production but on$351$.

While monitoring a telecommunications company, Pachidi observed a growing $352$on the recommendations made by AI, as workers begin to learn through the 'algorithm's eyes'. Meanwhile, staff are deterred from experimenting and using their own $353$, and are therefore prevented from achieving innovation.

To avoid the kind of situations which Pachidi observed, researchers are trying to make AI's decision-making process easier to comprehend, and to increase users' $354$with regard to the technology.

list.text: pressure
satisfaction
intuition
promotion
reliance
confidence
information

questions.analysis: 正确答案是G: information。题干中的“rely not on production but on”在原文中对应的是“dependent on data rather than on production”。“data”最直接的同义词是“information”，因此答案是G。

central_sentences.sentence: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy.
central_sentences.translation: 剑桥大学贾奇商学院的Stella Pachidi博士认为，最根本的变化之一是由于依赖数据而非生产的工作"算法化"——即所谓的知识经济。

target_sen.target_sen: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy.

syn_ary.synonym: dependent on data rather than on production

target_sen.target_sen: Dr Stella Pachidi from Cambridge Judge Business School believes that some of the most fundamental changes are happening as a result of the 'algorithmication' of jobs that are dependent on data rather than on production – the so-called knowledge economy.

questions.analysis: 题干中的“a growing 【 】on the recommendations made by AI”可以通过文章中的“a short-sighted view begins to creep into working practices whereby workers learn through the 'algorithm's eyes' and become dependent on its instructions”找到对应关系。这里的“creep into”与“growing”意思相近，而“dependent on”与“reliance”是同义词。因此，正确答案是“reliance”。

central_sentences.sentence: In cases like this, Pachidi believes, a short-sighted view begins to creep into working practices whereby workers learn through the 'algorithm's eyes' and become dependent on its instructions.
central_sentences.translation: 在这种情况下，帕奇迪认为，一种短视的观点开始潜入工作实践中，工人们通过"算法的眼睛"学习，并开始依赖于算法的指令。

target_sen.target_sen: The algorithm – usually built by external designers – often becomes the keeper of knowledge, she explains.
In cases like this, Pachidi believes, a short-sighted view begins to creep into working practices whereby workers learn through the 'algorithm's eyes' and become dependent on its instructions.

questions.analysis: 在题干中，"experimenting" 对应原文中的 "Alternative explorations"，"using their own" 对应 "human instinct"，"achieving innovation" 对应 "lead to progress and new ideas"。因此，正确答案是 "intuition"，因为它是 "human instinct" 的同义词。

central_sentences.sentence: Alternative explorations – where experimentation and human instinct lead to progress and new ideas – are effectively discouraged.
central_sentences.translation: 另类探索——即通过实验和人类直觉导致进步和新想法的方式——实际上被阻止了。

target_sen.target_sen: Alternative explorations – where experimentation and human instinct lead to progress and new ideas – are effectively discouraged.
Alternative explorations – where experimentation and human instinct lead to progress and new ideas – are effectively discouraged.

questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是“confidence”。题干中提到研究者的目标是让AI的决策过程更容易理解，并增加用户对技术的信任感。原文中提到的“make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent”与题干中的“increase users' ... with regard to the technology”相对应，而“trustworthy”指的就是增加用户的信任感，这与“confidence”相符。因此，正确答案是“confidence”。

central_sentences.sentence: Their objective is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent, so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made.
central_sentences.translation: 他们的目标是使AI技术更值得信赖和透明，以便组织和个人能够理解AI如何做出决策。

target_sen.target_sen: Their objective is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent, so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made.
Their objective is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent, so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made.

groups.title: List of people
groups.desc:
Questions 35–40

Look at the following statements (Questions 35–40) and the list of people below.
Match each statement with the correct person, A, B or C.
Move the correct answer, A, B or C, into boxes 35–40.

list.text: Stella Pachidi
Hamish Low
Ewan McGaughey

questions.title: Greater levels of automation will not result in lower employment.
questions.analysis: Hamish Low 认为自动化不会导致就业减少，而是会增加工作岗位数量，这与题干的意思完全一致。因此，正确答案是B. Hamish Low。通过识别关键词和核心观点，可以帮助学生更好地理解题意并做出正确的选择。

central_sentences.sentence: If in 30 years, half of 100 jobs are being carried out by robots, that doesn't mean we are left with just 50 jobs for humans. The number of jobs will increase: we would expect there to be 150 jobs.
central_sentences.translation: 如果30年后，100个工作中有一半由机器人完成，这并不意味着我们只剩下50个给人类的工作。工作数量反而会增加：我们预计会有150个工作岗位。

target_sen.target_sen: On the subject of job losses, Low believes the predictions are founded on a fallacy: 'It assumes that the number of jobs is fixed.
If in 30 years, half of 100 jobs are being carried out by robots, that doesn't mean we are left with just 50 jobs for humans.
The number of jobs will increase: we would expect there to be 150 jobs.'

questions.title: There are several reasons why AI is appealing to businesses.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是 A: Stella Pachidi。题干问的是为什么 AI 对企业有吸引力，而在文章中，Stella Pachidi 提到“组织被算法吸引，因为他们希望基于他们认为的‘完美信息’做出选择，同时减少成本并提高生产力。”这正是对题干问题的直接回答。

central_sentences.sentence: Organisations are attracted to using algorithms because they want to make choices based on what they consider is “perfect information”, as well as to reduce costs and enhance productivity
central_sentences.translation: 组织被算法吸引，因为他们希望基于他们认为的"完美信息"做出选择，同时减少成本并提高生产力。

target_sen.target_sen: 'Organisations are attracted to using algorithms because they want to make choices based on what they consider is “perfect information”, as well as to reduce costs and enhance productivity.'
'Organisations are attracted to using algorithms because they want to make choices based on what they consider is “perfect information”, as well as to reduce costs and enhance productivity.'

questions.title: AI's potential to transform people's lives has parallels with major cultural shifts which occurred in previous eras.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C. Ewan McGaughey。题干中提到的“AI's potential to transform people's lives has parallels with major cultural shifts which occurred in previous eras”与原文中Ewan McGaughey的描述最为契合。原文中提到的“industrial revolution”，“corporate revolution”和“third revolution”与题干中的“cultural shifts”和“previous eras”相对应，而“deliver humankind the capacity to live in a way”则与“transform people's lives”相对应。因此，答案是C. Ewan McGaughey。

central_sentences.sentence: Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced. But it will not only be one of technology. The next revolution will be social.
central_sentences.translation: 正如工业革命使人类摆脱了自给自足的农业，企业革命促进了大规模生产，现在第三次革命已经到来。但这不仅仅是一场技术革命，下一次革命将是一场社会革命。

syn_ary.synonym: deliver humankind the capacity to live in a way that nobody could have once imagined

target_sen.target_sen: They deliver humankind the capacity to live in a way that nobody could have once imagined,' he adds.
'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced.
'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced.
'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced.
'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced.
'Just as the industrial revolution brought people past subsistence agriculture, and the corporate revolution enabled mass production, a third revolution has been pronounced.

questions.title: It is important to be aware of the range of problems that AI causes.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A. Stella Pachidi，因为她在文章中明确提到需要“fully understand the dilemmas” that AI raises，这与题干中的“be aware of the range of problems that AI causes”意思相同。通过关键词对比和理解每个研究人员的主要观点，可以准确找到答案。

central_sentences.sentence: We need to make sure we fully understand the dilemmas that this new world raises regarding expertise, occupational boundaries and control.
central_sentences.translation: 我们需要确保充分理解这个新世界在专业知识、职业界限和控制方面带来的困境。

target_sen.target_sen: In the meantime, says Pachidi, 'We need to make sure we fully understand the dilemmas that this new world raises regarding expertise, occupational boundaries and control.'
Their objective is to make AI technologies more trustworthy and transparent, so that organisations and individuals understand how AI decisions are made.
In the meantime, says Pachidi, 'We need to make sure we fully understand the dilemmas that this new world raises regarding expertise, occupational boundaries and control.'

questions.title: People are going to follow a less conventional career path than in the past.
questions.analysis: 本题的正确答案是B. Hamish Low。题干中的“less conventional career path”对应文章中Hamish Low提到的“traditional trajectory... is a thing of the past”和“multistage employment life”。这些表达都表明未来的职业路径将不再是传统的单一职业生涯，而是多阶段的职业生涯。

central_sentences.sentence: Instead, he envisages a multistage employment life: one where retraining happens across the life course, and where multiple jobs and no job happen by choice at different stages.
central_sentences.translation: 相反，他预想未来的职业生涯将是多阶段的：在整个生命过程中会进行再培训，在不同阶段会出现多份工作或无工作的情况，这些都是由个人选择决定的。

target_sen.target_sen: Instead, he envisages a multistage employment life: one where retraining happens across the life course, and where multiple jobs and no job happen by choice at different stages.

questions.title: Authorities should take measures to ensure that there will be adequately paid work for everyone.
questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C.Ewan McGaughey。题干中提到“Authorities should take measures to ensure that there will be adequately paid work for everyone”，意思是“当局应该采取措施确保每个人都有足够支付的工作”。在原文中，Ewan McGaughey提到“governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security”，意思是“政府抓住机会改进政策，以确保良好的工作保障”。这两者的意思是相同的，都是在讨论政府应采取措施确保工作机会和工作保障。因此，正确答案是C.Ewan McGaughey。

central_sentences.sentence: If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security.
central_sentences.translation: 如果人工智能和机器人技术将导致工作发生变化，那么我希望看到政府抓住机会改善政策，以保障良好的工作保障。

target_sen.target_sen: He adds: 'If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security.

McGaughey's findings are a call to arms to leaders of organisations, governments and banks to pre-empt the coming changes with bold new policies that guarantee full employment, fair incomes and a thriving economic democracy.
He adds: 'If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security.
He adds: 'If there is going to be change to jobs as a result of AI and robotics then I'd like to see governments seizing the opportunity to improve policy to enforce good job security.
